summary:
- publisher=Splash247, title=One LNG transit, zero breakthrough in Hormuz crisis, directness=high, why=confirms isolated LNG transit occurred despite ongoing crisis, next=https://splash247.com/one-lng-transit-zero-breakthrough-in-hormuz-crisis/  
- publisher=gCaptain, title=Second Qatari LNG tanker heads through Hormuz to Pakistan as Iran war continues, directness=high, why=verifies second confirmed LNG transit (Al Kharaitiyat) on May 9, 2026, corroborated by LSEG data, next=https://gcaptain.com/second-qatari-lng-tanker-heads-through-hormuz-to-pakistan-as-iran-war-continues-data-shows/  
- publisher=gCaptain, title=Qatari LNG Tanker Sailing Towards Hormuz Strait, directness=high, why=earlier report (May 9) of same Al Kharaitiyat vessel’s movement, confirms origin (Ras Laffan) and destination (Port Qasim), next=https://gcaptain.com/qatari-lng-tanker-sailing-towards-hormuz-strait/  
- publisher=gCaptain, title=U.S. escorts first US-flagged ships through Hormuz as Iran warns of attack, directness=high, why=establishes U.S. military presence enabling limited transit, dated May 4, 2026, implies ongoing operational intervention, next=https://gcaptain.com/u-s-escorts-first-us-flagged-ships-through-hormuz-as-iran-warns-of-attack/  
- publisher=gCaptain, title=Trump says Iran ceasefire on ‘life support’ as Hormuz crisis deepens, directness=high, why=shows diplomatic stalemate persists despite minor transit, undermines near-term resolution, next=https://gcaptain.com/us-iran-far-apart-in-talks-to-end-war-and-reopen-hormuz/  
- publisher=gCaptain, title=Shipping traffic through Hormuz remains muted with no US-Iran deal in sight, directness=high, why=baseline context: traffic remains low despite isolated transits, no systemic reopening, next=https://gcaptain.com/shipping-traffic-through-hormuz-remains-muted-with-no-us-iran-deal-in-sight-data-shows/  
- publisher=Port of Los Angeles, title=Shipping won’t normalize without lasting Hormuz stability, directness=high, why=authoritative industry stakeholder links Hormuz status to global supply chain normalization, signals downstream impact, next=https://gcaptain.com/port-of-los-angeles-chief-says-shipping-wont-normalize-without-lasting-hormuz-stability/  
- publisher=Lloyd’s List, title=Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36% but voyages are longer, directness=medium, why=quantifies economic impact on tankers, implies rerouting and cost escalation, though date unverified, next=https://www.lloydslist.com/LL1157100/Hormuz-crisis-slashes-VLCC-volumes-by-36-but-voyages-are-longer
